title: impatiens mackeyana subsp. claeri
intro: Impatiens mackeyana subsp. claeri, also known as the Balsam Impatiens or Policres, is a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Impatiens mackeyana subsp. claeri include the treatment of fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
